title: Curriculum crosswalk model + code-level mapping to ≥ 2 frameworks (license-clean)
context: oer-science-labs aligns guides to curriculum standards, but standards frameworks have varied licensing that must be handled carefully (e.g. NGSS is copyright Achieve Inc.; many national curricula are Crown copyright or similarly restricted). This task defines the crosswalk data model and populates it for at least 2 frameworks using code + link + own rationale only — never embedding restricted full text. Depends on data-003 (curriculumAlignments schema field must exist) and content-007 (guides to align must exist).
objective: Define a curriculum crosswalk data model that maps guides to standards by standard code and link (plus own alignment rationale), structured to be additive for future frameworks. Implement code-level mappings for at least 2 curriculum frameworks (e.g. NGSS plus one national/regional framework). Verify and record each framework's license before creating any mapping; where full text reuse is not permitted, store code and link only.
acceptanceCriteria: Crosswalk data model is defined and version-controlled; structure is additive so new frameworks can be appended without breaking existing mappings
Mappings exist for at least 2 distinct curriculum frameworks (e.g. NGSS + one national/regional framework)
Each mapping records: framework name, standard code, link to the official standard statement, and an original written alignment rationale — no verbatim restricted standard text is embedded

Each framework's license is verified and recorded in the crosswalk metadata (e.g. NGSS: © Achieve, Inc., attribution required; or the applicable national curriculum license); a framework whose license cannot be cleared is referenced by code/link only
Mappings cover the guides published in content-007, with at least one alignment per guide per applicable framework
The crosswalk is machine-readable and can be used to generate discipline/standard filter options in the browse UI (code-008)
No verbatim restricted standard text appears anywhere in the crosswalk dataset or guide files
output: A pull request adding the curriculum crosswalk data model and license-clean code-level mappings to at least 2 frameworks, covering the guides in content-007, to the oer-science-labs repository.
